overall_summary: ElizaOS is transitioning toward Eliza v3 (version 2.x) with a strategic pivot from messaging to cryptographic AgentID and cross-chain financial autonomy. While technical progress on the v3 develop branch and new multi-chain plugins is steady, the community is currently managing significant token price volatility and emerging concerns regarding agent safety protocols.
key_facts: Eliza v3 (version 2.x) code is now available in the develop branch and integrated into the Milady project.
A new cross-chain swap plugin supporting over 10 networks was added via the MangoSwap pull request.
Project leadership clarified that ElizaOS is a multichain platform and is not moving exclusively to Ethereum.
The Eliza MCP server achieved an 'A' security grade via Loaditout for financial operation standards.
Strategic focus has shifted from standalone messaging to 'AgentID', a cryptographic identity framework for tracking agent trust.
PR #6709 was submitted to fix TOON action parameters and suppress noisy continuation loops for async tasks.
Active development is underway for plugin-anthropic to support Opus 4.x compatibility and ai-sdk v6 naming conventions.
The elizabao_ai project is transitioning to integration with elizacloud to create prediction market agents.
open_questions: How do you currently prevent your agent from doing something unsafe?
What's the stack lookin' like for the v3 agents?
Is there anyone looking for a skilled developer?
What exactly is the elizabao_ai project, and what can I as a user do there at this moment?

new_issues_prs.title: Fix/toon action params
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Critical fix for parameter extraction in TOON format and efficiency improvements for async task handling.

new_issues_prs.title: Plugin: MAXIA AI Marketplace
new_issues_prs.author: majorelalexis-stack
new_issues_prs.significance: Proposal for a unified AI-to-AI marketplace including GPU rentals and token swaps.

new_issues_prs.title: Plugin: SafeAgent — Token safety checks before trading
new_issues_prs.author: CryptoGenesisSecurity
new_issues_prs.significance: Multi-chain security tool to prevent agents from interacting with honeypots or rug pulls.

new_issues_prs.title: feat: add agent/ like starter in develop
new_issues_prs.author: odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces a new REPL harness and local development workflow using submodules.

overall_focus.claim: The project is prioritizing the transition to a 'Secure Financial Foundation' and 'AgentID' cryptographic identity over basic messaging protocols.

discord_updates.summary: Focus on Eliza v3 (2.x) testing and release preparation. Developers raised unresolved concerns about implementing hard safety guardrails for autonomous agent actions.
Clarifications on the multichain nature of the project and updates on the elizabao_ai sub-project. Introduction of new community developer 0xcrackedev.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed significant frustration and negative sentiment regarding the 95% decline in ELISA token price and overall market conditions.
Positive reports on a successful first agent deployment using Hatcher.host's one-click process with Groq integration.

strategic_insights.theme: Pivot to Agent Identity over Messaging
strategic_insights.insight: By deprioritizing protocols like XMTP in favor of AgentID, the project is positioning itself as an infrastructure layer for verifiable agent behavior rather than just a chatbot framework.
implications_or_questions: How will this affect existing plugins that rely solely on standard messaging?
Does this create a barrier for entry for non-technical developers?

strategic_insights.insight: The 'cold-start' trust problem between independent agents is a primary research focus, with potential solutions involving on-chain credentials and Base L2 attestation.
implications_or_questions: Will the required on-chain history create centralizing effects for older agents?

market_analysis.observation: Community sentiment in general discussion channels is skeptical of crypto promises due to unmaterialized announcements and 'very bad' trading conditions.
market_analysis.relevance: High; persistent negative sentiment may affect developer retention and community participation.

market_analysis.observation: BitMart representative reached out to the core team to discuss listing cooperation.
market_analysis.relevance: Potential for increased liquidity and market visibility if a listing occurs.
